---
framework: opencart
version: "3.x"
last_updated: "2026-04-28"
author: "@neotherapper"
status: official
---

# OpenCart 3.x — Tech Pack

OpenCart is a popular open-source PHP e-commerce platform with approximately 180,000 live
stores globally. OC 3.x is the current major branch and runs on a custom MVC framework
(not Symfony or Laravel). It is widely deployed on shared hosting due to its low resource
requirements. OpenCart is especially prevalent among small-to-medium retailers in the UK,
India, Southeast Asia, and Eastern Europe. The platform's architecture centers on a single
`index.php` entry point where all routing is driven by the `route=` query parameter.

## 1. Fingerprinting Signals

| Signal | Type | Value | Confidence |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `OCSESSID` cookie | Cookie | Name exact match | Definitive |
| `route=` in URL query string | URL pattern | `?route=controller/method` | Definitive |
| `window.opencart` JS global | JS global | Object present in inline scripts | High (OC 3.x+) |
| `/catalog/view/theme/` in HTML src | HTML substring | Script/link src contains path | High |
| `/image/cache/` in HTML img src | HTML substring | Product image path with cache | High |
| `route=common/home` on homepage | URL pattern | Confirmed on `?route=common/home` | High |
| Generator meta tag | HTML meta | `OpenCart` or `opencart.com` in HTML comment | Medium |
| Powered-by HTML comment | HTML comment | `Powered By OpenCart` (many themes remove it) | Low |
| `oc-` prefixed cookies | Cookie | Name prefix match | Medium (older OC) |
| Wappalyzer result | MCP | `"OpenCart"` | Definitive |

**Version extraction:**
```bash
# Check generator meta or HTML comment:
curl -s {site} | grep -i "opencart"

# Window global (OC 3.x):
curl -s {site} | grep -o 'window\.opencart[^;]*'

# Admin panel (if accessible):
curl -s {site}/admin/ | grep -oP 'OpenCart \K[\d.]+'

# From changelog (if exposed):
curl -s {site}/CHANGELOG.md
curl -s {site}/INSTALL.txt
```

## 2. Default API Surfaces

### Built-in REST API (must be enabled in admin — disabled by default)

The OC 3.x API requires an API user and key configured in Admin > System > API. All API
requests require a valid `api_token` obtained by calling `api/login` first.

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/index.php?route=api/login` | POST | API credentials | Returns `api_token`; required before all other API calls |
| `/index.php?route=api/cart/add` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Add product to cart |
| `/index.php?route=api/cart/edit` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Edit cart item quantity |
| `/index.php?route=api/cart/remove` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Remove cart item |
| `/index.php?route=api/cart/products` | GET | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Cart contents |
| `/index.php?route=api/shipping/address` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Set shipping address |
| `/index.php?route=api/shipping/methods` | GET | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Available shipping methods |
| `/index.php?route=api/shipping/method` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Select a shipping method |
| `/index.php?route=api/payment/address` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Set payment address |
| `/index.php?route=api/payment/methods` | GET | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Available payment methods |
| `/index.php?route=api/payment/method` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Select a payment method |
| `/index.php?route=api/order/add` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Create order |
| `/index.php?route=api/order/edit` | POST | `api_token` | Edit existing order |
| `/index.php?route=api/order/info` | GET | `api_token` | Order details |
| `/index.php?route=api/order/history` | POST | `api_token` | Add order history entry |
| `/index.php?route=api/customer` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Set customer info for session |
| `/index.php?route=api/currency` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Set active currency |
| `/index.php?route=api/coupon` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Apply coupon code |
| `/index.php?route=api/voucher` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Apply voucher code |
| `/index.php?route=api/reward` | POST | `api_token` + `OCSESSID` | Apply reward points |

### Standard controller routes (always active, no auth required)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/index.php?route=common/home` | GET | None | Homepage — confirms OC routing |
| `/index.php?route=product/product&product_id={N}` | GET | None | Single product page |
| `/index.php?route=product/category&path={N}` | GET | None | Category listing |
| `/index.php?route=product/search&search={q}` | GET | None | Search results |
| `/index.php?route=product/search&search={q}&limit=100` | GET | None | Broad search — up to 100 results |
| `/index.php?route=product/manufacturer` | GET | None | All brands/manufacturers |
| `/index.php?route=product/manufacturer/info&manufacturer_id={N}` | GET | None | Products by brand |
| `/index.php?route=product/special` | GET | None | On-sale / discounted products |
| `/index.php?route=checkout/cart` | GET | None | Cart page (HTML) |
| `/index.php?route=checkout/cart/add` | POST | None | Add to cart AJAX — returns JSON |
| `/index.php?route=checkout/cart/remove` | POST | None | Remove from cart AJAX — returns JSON |
| `/index.php?route=checkout/cart/edit` | POST | None | Edit cart AJAX — returns JSON |
| `/index.php?route=account/wishlist` | GET | Customer session | Wishlist (requires login) |
| `/index.php?route=extension/feed/google_base` | GET | None | Google Shopping XML feed (if enabled in admin) |
| `/index.php?route=extension/feed/google_sitemap` | GET | None | XML sitemap (OC 3.x built-in feed extension) |
| `/sitemap.xml` | GET | None | Sitemap via friendly URL (if SEO URLs + rewrite enabled) |

### URL rewriting (friendly URLs — when SEO URLs are enabled)

When SEO-friendly URLs are enabled in admin, `?route=product/product&product_id=N`
transforms to `/{category-slug}/{product-slug}`. Both forms are always accessible — use
`?route=` form for reliable probing regardless of SEO URL configuration.

## 3. Config / Constants Locations

| Location | What's there | How to access |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `window.opencart` inline JS | Base URL, theme name, language, currency, customer state | JS eval / grep page source (OC 3.x+) |
| `/catalog/view/theme/{theme}/javascript/` | Theme JS path — reveals active theme name | HTML src attribute scrape |
| `/image/cache/{W}x{H}/catalog/` | Product image paths — reveals filenames and dimensions | HTML img src scrape |
| `OCSESSID` cookie value | Session ID — required for cart/checkout API calls | Response header / cookie jar |
| `config/config.php` | DB credentials, secret, site URL | Source only — blocked in production |
| `config/catalog.php` | Catalog-layer config | Source only |
| Product page `<script type="application/ld+json">` | Name, price, currency, availability, image | HTML scrape — no auth |
| `meta[name=description]` | Product/category description | HTML scrape |

## 4. Auth Patterns

| Pattern | Location | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| API token | `POST /index.php?route=api/login` → `api_token` | Required for all `/api/` endpoints; pass as `&api_token={token}` |
| Customer session | `OCSESSID` cookie | Front-office session; set on first visit; required for cart state in API |
| Admin session | `/admin/` login form | Standard PHP session; admin is always at `/admin/` (not randomized) |
| `PHPSESSID` cookie | Cookie | Standard PHP session ID; may be present alongside `OCSESSID` |

**API token acquisition and usage:**
```bash
# Step 1: Get API token (credentials from Admin > System > API)
curl -X POST "{site}/index.php?route=api/login" \
  -d "username=Default&key={api_key}"
# Returns: {"success":"Success","api_token":"xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"}

# Step 2: Use token for subsequent API calls (also pass OCSESSID cookie)
curl "{site}/index.php?route=api/cart/products&api_token={token}" \
  -b "OCSESSID={session_id}"

# Step 3: Add a product to cart
curl -X POST "{site}/index.php?route=api/cart/add" \
  -b "OCSESSID={session_id}" \
  -d "api_token={token}&product_id=1&quantity=1"
```

**Cart AJAX (no API token — front-office, no auth):**
```bash
# Add to cart via front-office AJAX controller:
curl -X POST "{site}/index.php?route=checkout/cart/add" \
  -b "OCSESSID={session_id}" \
  -d "product_id=1&quantity=1"
# Returns JSON: {"success":"...", "total":"...", "products":N}

# Remove from cart:
curl -X POST "{site}/index.php?route=checkout/cart/remove" \
  -b "OCSESSID={session_id}" \
  -d "key={cart_key}"
```

## 5. JS Bundle Patterns

| Path | Content |
|------|---------|
| `/catalog/view/javascript/jquery/jquery-3.x.x.min.js` | jQuery (OC bundles its own copy) |
| `/catalog/view/javascript/common.js` | Core site JS — AJAX cart handlers, alert/notification logic |
| `/catalog/view/theme/{theme}/javascript/common.js` | Theme-overriding common.js |
| `/catalog/view/theme/default/javascript/jquery-ui/` | jQuery UI (bundled with default theme) |
| `/catalog/view/theme/{theme}/javascript/` | All theme JS files |
| `/catalog/view/theme/journal3/javascript/` | Journal3 theme JS (most popular paid theme) |
| `/catalog/view/javascript/bootstrap/` | Bootstrap bundled with OC |

Check each bundle URL + `.map` suffix for source maps (uncommon in stock OC installs, but
custom themes compiled with webpack may expose them).

## 6. Source Map Patterns

OpenCart core JS does **not** include source maps in production. Custom themes (especially
Journal3 and other commercial themes compiled with webpack or Gulp) may expose maps.

```bash
# Check theme JS for source maps:
curl -I {site}/catalog/view/theme/{theme}/javascript/theme.min.js.map

# Check common.js for sourceMappingURL comment:
curl -s {site}/catalog/view/javascript/common.js | grep "sourceMappingURL"

# Journal3 theme — webpack bundles:
curl -I {site}/catalog/view/theme/journal3/javascript/journal3.min.js.map
```

Retrieve the active theme name from `/catalog/view/theme/{theme}/` path patterns in HTML
src attributes.

## 7. Common Extensions & Themes

| Extension / Theme | API / endpoint it adds | Detection signal |
|-------------------|----------------------|-----------------|
| Journal3 (paid theme) | No new API routes; heavily customizes JS | `/catalog/view/theme/journal3/` in HTML src |
| OpenCart Marketplace extensions | Custom `route=extension/` controller paths | `/admin/index.php?route=marketplace/` |
| OCMOD modifications | No new endpoints; patches core files in-place | `system/storage/modification/` paths may appear in errors |
| Google Base Feed (built-in) | `GET /index.php?route=extension/feed/google_base` | 200 response with `<?xml` and `<rss` (must be enabled in admin) |
| Product Reviews module | `POST /index.php?route=product/review` (AJAX) | Review form on product pages |
| Quick Checkout / OnePageCheckout | Custom `route=checkout/` paths | Non-standard checkout URL in HTML |
| AjaxFilter / Layered Navigation | `GET /index.php?route=extension/module/ajaxfilter` | Filter sidebar + AJAX category results |
| Currency Switcher | `POST /index.php?route=common/currency` | Currency form on homepage |
| Language Switcher | `POST /index.php?route=common/language` | Language selector in header |

## 8. Known Public Data

| Endpoint | Data | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| `GET /index.php?route=product/special` | Discounted/on-sale products (name, price, image) | Always public; richest unauthenticated product listing |
| `GET /index.php?route=product/manufacturer` | All brand/manufacturer names and IDs | Always public |
| `GET /index.php?route=extension/feed/google_base` | Full product XML feed — name, description, price, image URL, SKU, model, EAN/UPC, availability | Richest public data source; complete catalog when enabled in admin |
| `GET /index.php?route=product/search&search=a&limit=100` | Broad search results — up to 100 products | Searchable without auth |
| `GET /index.php?route=extension/feed/google_sitemap` | XML sitemap — product URLs with last-modified dates | Built-in OC 3.x feed extension; must be enabled in admin |
| `GET /sitemap.xml` | Sitemap via friendly URL | Works when SEO URLs + mod_rewrite are enabled |
| Product page `<script type="application/ld+json">` | JSON-LD: name, price, currency, availability, SKU, image | Every product page; no auth |
| `/image/cache/{W}x{H}/catalog/` img paths | Product image filenames and dimension variants | Scraped from HTML img src attributes |

## 9. Probe Checklist

Run these in order. Record result (✓ 200 / ✗ 403 / – 404) for each.

- [ ] `HEAD {site}` — check `Set-Cookie` for `OCSESSID` cookie (definitive fingerprint)
- [ ] `GET {site}` — grep for `route=`, `/catalog/view/theme/`, `/image/cache/`, `window.opencart`; confirm active theme from src path
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=common/home` — confirm OC routing is active (identical to homepage)
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=product/special` — on-sale products (always public; good catalog sample)
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=product/manufacturer` — brand/manufacturer list with IDs
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=product/search&search=a&limit=100` — broad search results
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=product/product&product_id=1` — probe first product (try IDs 1–10 if 404)
- [ ] Check product page for `<script type="application/ld+json">` — JSON-LD product data (name, price, SKU)
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=extension/feed/google_base` — Google Shopping XML feed (200+XML=enabled; 404=disabled)
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=extension/feed/google_sitemap` — XML sitemap feed (200+XML=enabled; 404=disabled)
- [ ] `GET {site}/sitemap.xml` — sitemap via friendly URL (if SEO URLs + mod_rewrite enabled)
- [ ] `POST {site}/index.php?route=checkout/cart/add` with `product_id=1&quantity=1` — front-office cart AJAX (returns JSON)
- [ ] `POST {site}/index.php?route=api/login` with `username=Default&key=test` — API status: JSON error=enabled, HTML=disabled
- [ ] `GET {site}/admin/` — admin panel (expect OC login form; confirms `/admin/` path is active)
- [ ] Check `/catalog/view/theme/` path in HTML src — identify active theme name
- [ ] Check `/image/cache/` paths in HTML img src — reveals product image dimensions and filenames
- [ ] `GET {site}/index.php?route=product/category&path=1` — probe first category (try IDs 1–20)

## 10. Gotchas

- **The REST API is disabled by default.** A 200 from `POST /index.php?route=api/login`
  returning JSON means the API is enabled. An HTML response (the homepage or a 404 page)
  means the API is disabled or the route doesn't exist. Most production stores never
  configure the API — do not treat this as a blocker; use front-office routes instead.

- **`route=` is the entire routing mechanism.** Every controller action in OpenCart follows
  `?route=module/method` — understanding this pattern unlocks all other endpoints. When
  SEO-friendly URLs transform these into path-based URLs, the `?route=` form still always
  works and is more reliable for automated probing.

- **Friendly URLs and `?route=` coexist permanently.** Enabling SEO URLs in admin adds
  path-based aliases (e.g. `/{slug}`), but the `?route=product/product&product_id=N` form
  is never disabled. Always use `?route=` form for probing.

- **The Google Base feed is the richest public data source.** When enabled,
  `GET /index.php?route=extension/feed/google_base` returns an RSS/XML document containing
  name, description, price, image URL, SKU, model, EAN/UPC, link, and availability for
  every active product. This requires no authentication and is common on stores targeting
  Google Shopping. It is the single most valuable public endpoint in OpenCart.

- **Admin is always at `/admin/` — never randomized.** Unlike PrestaShop (which randomizes
  the admin path), OpenCart's admin is permanently at `/admin/`. This is a known security
  weakness. Default credentials (`admin`/`admin`) are frequently left unchanged on hobbyist
  or poorly maintained stores — always probe the admin login form.

- **`OCSESSID` is required for cart/checkout API calls.** The API session is tied to the
  `OCSESSID` cookie value. Obtain a fresh session ID with a HEAD or GET request to the
  homepage, capture the cookie, and pass it with every API call. Changing or omitting it
  resets cart state and may cause API errors.

- **The API token is separate from admin credentials.** The API system has its own user
  accounts (Admin > System > API), distinct from the admin panel login. The default API
  user is named `Default` and has no key until one is generated in the admin.

- **`/image/cache/` exposes product image filenames and dimension variants.** OpenCart
  generates resized copies of uploaded images in `/image/cache/{W}x{H}/catalog/`. The
  filenames in HTML img src attributes reveal the original product image paths and the
  dimension variants configured for that store's theme.

- **OpenCart 2.x and 3.x share most probe patterns.** The architecture is nearly identical
  across 2.x and 3.x. The main 3.x structural difference is the `extension/` routing
  prefix for feeds and modules (e.g. `route=extension/feed/google_base`). If
  `/catalog/view/theme/` path patterns and `OCSESSID` are present but the
  `extension/feed/` routes return 404, the store may be running 2.x — probe
  `route=feed/google_base` (the older direct route) as a fallback.

- **Extension routes are not discoverable from URLs alone.** OCMOD extensions patch core
  files in-place. Third-party modules may add custom `route=` controllers under
  `catalog/controller/` that have no standard naming convention. Enumerate by reading
  theme JS for `$.ajax` and `fetch(` calls pointing to custom `route=` values.

- **The front-office cart AJAX (`route=checkout/cart/add`) returns JSON.** This is distinct
   from the REST API cart endpoint. No token is required — only the `OCSESSID` cookie. This
   is always active and the most reliable cart manipulation method regardless of API status.
- **Journal3 is the most popular commercial theme** and significantly modifies the frontend
   JS structure. Detect it via `/catalog/view/theme/journal3/` in HTML src paths. Its JS
   bundles are compiled and may differ substantially from the default theme's common.js.
- **The Google Base feed and sitemap must be explicitly enabled.** Go to Admin > Extensions >
   Feeds, edit Google Base (or Google Sitemap), and set Status to Enabled. On a fresh OC
   install both feeds return a 404. A 200 with `<?xml` content confirms the feed is live.
   The correct OC 3.x routes are `extension/feed/google_base` and
   `extension/feed/google_sitemap` — not the shorter `feed/google_base` form used in
   older 2.x installations.
- **`bestseller` and `latest` are homepage modules, not page routes.** In OC 3.x,
   bestseller and latest-products listings are rendered via `extension/module/bestseller`
   and `extension/module/latest` as homepage widget modules — there is no standalone
   `route=product/bestseller` or `route=product/latest` page controller in core OC 3.x.
   Probing those routes returns a 404. Use `route=product/special` for a real standalone
   product listing page.

## 11. GitHub Code Search Patterns

Use these queries on GitHub to find custom endpoints, plugin code, and configuration examples for this framework.

### Framework-Specific Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `"$this->model_catalog" language:php` | OpenCart catalog model usage |
| `"index.php?route=" language:php` | OpenCart route parameters |
| `"controller/" language:php path:controller/` | OpenCart controller definitions |
| `"oc_" language:php` | OpenCart hook and variable prefixes |

### Example Queries for OpenCart

```bash
# Search for custom API routes/endpoints
site:github.com "OpenCart" "api" filetype:php "route="

# Search for authentication patterns
site:github.com "OpenCart" "auth" "api_token" language:php

# Search for configuration files with endpoint definitions
site:github.com "OpenCart" "index.php" "endpoint" language:php

# Search for custom post types, taxonomies, or extensions
site:github.com "OpenCart" "module" "custom" language:php
```

## 12. Framework-Specific Google Dorks

Use these Google search queries to discover exposed endpoints, configuration files, and documentation for this framework.

### Discovery Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `site:{domain} inurl:/admin/` | OpenCart admin panel paths |
| `site:{domain} inurl:index.php?route=` | OpenCart controller routes |
| `site:{domain} "opencart" "api"` | OpenCart API endpoint references |
| `site:{domain} inurl:index.php?route=checkout/cart` | OpenCart cart AJAX endpoints |

### Complete Dork List for OpenCart

```
# API endpoints
site:{domain} inurl:index.php?route=api/
site:{domain} inurl:index.php?route=product/product
site:{domain} inurl:index.php?route=checkout/cart

# Framework-specific paths
site:{domain} inurl:/admin/
site:{domain} inurl:index.php?route=common/home

# Configuration files
site:{domain} filetype:php "opencart" "version"
site:{domain} filetype:php "config/config.php"

# Documentation/leaks
site:{domain} "OpenCart" "api" "endpoint"
site:{domain} "OCSESSID" "cookie"

# Admin/debug paths
site:{domain} inurl:/admin/
site:{domain} inurl:index.php?route=api/login
```

## 13. Cross-Cutting OSINT Patterns

These patterns apply across frameworks and should be checked for any detected technology.

### Favicon Hashing

Identify technology stack by hashing favicon and searching Shodan/Censys for same stack:

```bash
# Get favicon hash (mmh3 hash of favicon content)
curl -s "https://{domain}/favicon.ico" | python3 -c "
import sys, hashlib, base64
data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
# Simple mmh3 hash simulation using Python
import mmh3 2>/dev/null || pip install mmh3
# Or use: python3 -c "import mmh3; print(mmh3.hash(data))"
print('Favicon hash needed for Shodan search: icon_hash')
"

# Search Shodan for same favicon (indicates shadow IT subdomains)
# site:shodan.io search: icon_hash:{hash}
```

**What it reveals:** Hidden subdomains running same framework stack as main site.

### Source Map Discovery

Check for source maps across all JS bundles:

```bash
# Extract all JS bundle URLs from HTML
curl -s "https://{domain}/" | grep -oP 'src="[^"]+\.js[^"]*"' | grep -oP '"[^"]+' | tr -d '"' > js_urls.txt

# Check each for .map file
while read url; do
  map_url="${url}.map"
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${map_url}")
  [ "$status" = "200" ] && echo "SOURCE MAP: ${map_url}"
done < js_urls.txt
```

**Build tool patterns:**
| Build Tool | Source Map Pattern | Detection |
|------------|-------------------|------------|
| Webpack | `{bundle}.js.map` or `//# sourceMappingURL=` | Check response header `X-SourceMap` |
| Vite | `{name}-[hash].js.map` | Vite manifest `manifest.json` |
| Rollup | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| esbuild | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| Next.js | `/_next/static/chunks/*.js.map` | Only if `productionBrowserSourceMaps: true` |

### Tech Stack → API Pattern Mapping

Auto-map detected frameworks to likely endpoint patterns:

| Framework | Common API Patterns |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Next.js | `/api/*`, `/_next/data/*`, `/api/auth/*`, `/api/trpc/*` |
| WordPress | `/wp-json/*`, `/wp-json/wp/v2/*`, `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` |
| Shopify | `/api/2024-10/graphql.json`, `/products.json`, `/collections.json` |
| Rails | `/api/v1/*`, `/assets/*`, `/users/sign_in` |
| Laravel | `/api/*`, `/livewire/message/*`, `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` |
| Strapi | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/api/upload*` |
| Magento | `/rest/V1/*`, `/pub/static/*` |
| Django | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/accounts/*` |

When Phase 3 detects a framework, use this table to prioritize Phase 5/6/7 probes.

### Email Naming Convention Analysis

Extract emails from theHarvester/GitHub results to predict internal subdomains:

```bash
# Sample emails found: john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com
# Predicted subdomains: mail.example.com, smtp.example.com, exchange.example.com

# Common patterns:
# first.last@ → internal.example.com, mail.example.com
# firstinitial+last@ → owa.example.com, outlook.example.com
```

**Add to Phase 9 session brief:** Note email patterns and predicted subdomains.
